paper_title: TabTransformer: Tabular Data Modeling Using Contextual Embeddings
authors: Huang, Xin; Khetan, Ashish; Cvitkovic, Milan; Karnin, Zohar
query_context: attention mechanisms for tabular data — exploring whether contextual embeddings improve feature representation

key_claims: Contextual embeddings from Transformers outperform context-free MLP embeddings by 1.0% AUC
TabTransformer matches GBDT performance on 15 datasets
Contextual embeddings are robust against missing and noisy data
Pre-training via RTD/MLM yields 2.1% AUC lift in semi-supervised setting
findings.problem_addressed: Deep learning models for tabular data underperform tree-based methods like GBDT
findings.proposed_approach: Apply multi-head self-attention Transformer layers on categorical feature embeddings to create contextual embeddings

query_context: Need detailed architecture info for potential implementation of attention over tabular features

findings.method_summary: TabTransformer embeds each categorical feature via column-specific lookup tables with a shared column identifier. These parametric embeddings are passed through N=6 Transformer layers (8 attention heads, d=32) to produce contextual embeddings, which are concatenated with continuous features and fed to an MLP for prediction.
findings.architecture: Column Embedding → 6x Transformer Layers (8 heads, d=32) → Concat with continuous → MLP (4l, 2l)
key_findings: Semantically similar categorical features cluster together in contextual embedding space
Robustness to noise: TabTransformer degrades more gracefully than MLP as noise increases
Pre-training with RTD outperforms MLM on smaller unlabeled datasets
Linear probes on layer outputs show embeddings become progressively more effective
limitations: Only handles categorical features through attention; continuous features bypass Transformer
Pre-training benefit diminishes with small unlabeled datasets
findings.relevance_to_project: Core concept of applying attention to tabular feature embeddings is directly relevant to our TopAttention architecture

query_context: Extract exact equations for attention mechanism and loss function for implementation

equations.name: Column Embedding
equations.context: Section 2 — column embedding with unique identifier

definitions.definition: An embedding transformed through successive Transformer layers that aggregates context from all other feature embeddings, as opposed to context-free parametric embeddings from lookup tables
A parametric embedding for categorical features that concatenates a column-specific identifier c_φi with a class-specific vector w_φij, enabling the model to distinguish features across columns

factual_supports.claim: TabTransformer outperforms baseline MLP on 14 out of 15 datasets with an average 1.0% gain in AUC
Contextual embeddings show that semantically similar features cluster together in embedding space, while MLP embeddings show no such pattern
Pre-training with RTD improves semi-supervised AUC by 2.1% over state-of-the-art on large datasets
